data.title: Trump's tariffs fail to shrink trade gap as July's deficit hits $78.3B, worst since March 2025. Imports surge, exports stall.
data.description: Despite President Donald Trump's efforts to use tariffs to reduce the U.S. trade deficit, recent data reveals that these measures have not been successful. In July, the trade deficit widened to $78.3 billion, a significant increase from $59.1 billion in June, as reported by the Bureau of Economic Analysis. This figure was higher than the expected $75.7 billion shortfall and the largest monthly trade gap since March 2025. The trade deficit is now almost the same as a year ago, indicating that the tariffs implemented by Trump have not had the desired impact of reducing imports and protecting American industries. The increase in the trade gap in July was primarily driven by a significant 5.9% rise in imports, reaching $358.8 billion. Export growth, on the other hand, remained stagnant.
